opinion.h4: REED v. ILLINOIS. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 282. Decided October 10, 1966.
opinion.p: William G. Clark, Attorney General of Illinois, and Richard A. Michael and Philip J. Rock, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
TREFFRY ET AL. v. TAYLOR, DIRECTOR OF LICENSES FOR THE STATE OF WASHINGTON, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WASHINGTON. No. 291. Decided October 10, 1966.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
